---
title: "Week 7: Feb 23 - Mar 1, 2026"
subtitle: "Unit III Transition: Request Handling to Session Tracking"
---

## Week Overview

This week continues Unit III by covering practical request handling in servlets and introducing state management with cookies.

**Course Outcome:** CO-3 (K3 - Application)  
**Duration:** 1 week (3 lectures + 1 lab)  
**Lab:** Browser Information, Objects, and Script Reuse  
**Home Assignment 2 Due:** Feb 25

::: {.callout-tip icon=false}
## Quick Practice Quizzes (Lectures 19-21)
Use the [Quiz Hub](/quiz/index.qmd) or jump directly to:

- [Lecture 19 Quiz](/quiz/lecture-19-quiz.qmd)
- [Lecture 20 Quiz](/quiz/lecture-20-quiz.qmd)
- [Lecture 21 Quiz](/quiz/lecture-21-quiz.qmd)
:::

---

## Lecture 19: Handling HTTP GET & POST Requests {#lecture-19}

::: {.callout-note icon=false}
## Lecture Materials
[{{< fa list-check >}} Take Quiz](/quiz/lecture-19-quiz.qmd)
[{{< fa desktop >}} View Lecture](/lectures/lecture-19.qmd){target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"}
```{=html}
<div class="ratio ratio-16x9 mb-3">
  <iframe class="lecture-preview-frame" src="/lectures/lecture-19.html" title="Lecture presentation preview" loading="lazy" style="border: 1px solid #343a40;" allowfullscreen></iframe>
</div>
```
:::

### Key Concepts
- Purpose of `doGet()` and `doPost()` in servlet development
- Reading parameters using `request.getParameter()`
- Designing safe request processing flows

### Typical Example
```java
protected void doPost(HttpServletRequest request, HttpServletResponse response)
    throws ServletException, IOException {
  String username = request.getParameter("username");
  response.getWriter().println("Welcome " + username);
}
```

---

## Lecture 20: Redirecting Requests & Request Dispatching {#lecture-20}

::: {.callout-note icon=false}
## Lecture Materials
[{{< fa list-check >}} Take Quiz](/quiz/lecture-20-quiz.qmd)
[{{< fa desktop >}} View Lecture](/lectures/lecture-20.qmd){target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"}
```{=html}
<div class="ratio ratio-16x9 mb-3">
  <iframe class="lecture-preview-frame" src="/lectures/lecture-20.html" title="Lecture presentation preview" loading="lazy" style="border: 1px solid #343a40;" allowfullscreen></iframe>
</div>
```
:::

### Key Concepts
- Difference between server-side forward and client-side redirect
- Correct use of `RequestDispatcher` and `sendRedirect()`
- Request attribute handling during navigation

### Decision Rule
- Use `forward()` when request attributes are needed on the next page
- Use `sendRedirect()` after successful form submissions

---

## Lecture 21: Session Tracking using Cookies {#lecture-21}

::: {.callout-note icon=false}
## Lecture Materials
[{{< fa list-check >}} Take Quiz](/quiz/lecture-21-quiz.qmd)
[{{< fa desktop >}} View Lecture](/lectures/lecture-21.qmd){target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"}
```{=html}
<div class="ratio ratio-16x9 mb-3">
  <iframe class="lecture-preview-frame" src="/lectures/lecture-21.html" title="Lecture presentation preview" loading="lazy" style="border: 1px solid #343a40;" allowfullscreen></iframe>
</div>
```
:::

### Key Concepts
- Why session tracking is required in HTTP applications
- Creating and reading cookies in servlet-based apps
- Security and expiry considerations

### Example
```java
Cookie c = new Cookie("username", "student1");
c.setMaxAge(3600);
response.addCookie(c);
```

---

## Lab 5: Browser Information, Objects, and Script Reuse

### Objectives
- Inspect browser/client details from request objects
- Practice reusable JavaScript modules and utility methods
- Integrate dynamic client behaviour with server-side flow

### Activities
1. Build a page that shows browser details and request headers.
2. Store a temporary user value using cookies.
3. Add reusable JavaScript functions for validation/formatting.
4. Test both success and failure flows.

---

## Key Takeaways

✅ `doGet()` and `doPost()` solve different request needs  
✅ Forward and redirect are both essential but used in different scenarios  
✅ Cookies provide lightweight session tracking support

## Next Week

Continue Unit III with:
- Lecture 22: Session Tracking with `HttpSession`
- Lecture 23: JSP Introduction and implicit objects
- Lecture 24: JSP directives, actions, and custom tags
